input_text: Find the first name and gender of student who have more than one pet.
question: Find the first name and gender of student who have more than one pet.
sql: SELECT T1.fname ,  T1.sex FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid GROUP BY T1.stuid HAVING count(*)  >  1
cypher:
MATCH (T1:`pets_1.Student`)-[T2:`pets_1.Has_Pet`]-()
WITH T1.Sex AS Sex, T1.Fname AS Fname, count(*) AS count, T1.StuID AS StuID
WHERE count  > 1
RETURN Fname,Sex
text2sql_target: SELECT T1.fname ,  T1.sex FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid GROUP BY T1.stuid HAVING count(*)  >  1

WHERE count  > 1
RETURN Fname,Sex

input_text: Find the last name of the student who has a cat that is age 3.
question: Find the last name of the student who has a cat that is age 3.
sql: SELECT T1.lname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pet_age  =  3 AND T3.pettype  =  'cat'
cypher:
MATCH (T1:`pets_1.Student`)-[T2:`pets_1.Has_Pet`]-(T3:`pets_1.Pets`)
WHERE T3.pet_age = 3 AND T3.PetType = 'cat'
RETURN T1.LName
text2sql_target: SELECT T1.lname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pet_age  =  3 AND T3.pettype  =  'cat'

input_text: Which model of the car has the minimum horsepower?
question: Which model of the car has the minimum horsepower?
sql: SELECT T1.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId  =  T2.Id ORDER BY T2.horsepower ASC LIMIT 1;
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`car_1.cars_data`)-[:`car_1.cars_data_HAS_car_1.car_names`]->(T1:`car_1.car_names`)
RETURN T1.Model
ORDER BY T2.Horsepower ASC
LIMIT 1

input_text: Which distinct car models are the produced after 1980?
question: Which distinct car models are the produced after 1980?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.model FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.model  =  T2.model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T3 ON T2.MakeId  =  T3.id WHERE T3.year  >  1980;
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`car_1.car_names`)-[:`car_1.car_names_HAS_car_1.model_list`]->(T1:`car_1.model_list`),(T3:`car_1.cars_data`)-[:`car_1.cars_data_HAS_car_1.car_names`]->(T2:`car_1.car_names`)
WHERE T3.Year > 1980
RETURN DISTINCT T1.Model

input_text: Which of the countries has the most car makers? List the country name.
question: Which of the countries has the most car makers? List the country name.
sql: SELECT T2.CountryName FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN COUNTRIES AS T2 ON T1.Country  =  T2.CountryId GROUP BY T1.Country ORDER BY Count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
cypher:
MATCH (T1:`car_1.car_makers`)-[:`car_1.car_makers_HAS_car_1.countries`]->(T2:`car_1.countries`)
WITH T1.Country as Country, count(*) AS cnt, T2.CountryName as CountryName
RETURN CountryName
ORDER BY cnt DESC,Country DESC
LIMIT 1
text2sql_target: SELECT T2.CountryName FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN COUNTRIES AS T2 ON T1.Country  =  T2.CountryId GROUP BY T1.Country ORDER BY Count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

WITH T1.Country as Country, count(*) AS cnt, T2.CountryName as CountryName
RETURN CountryName
ORDER BY cnt DESC,Country DESC
LIMIT 1

input_text: For the cars with 4 cylinders, which model has the largest horsepower?
question: For the cars with 4 cylinders, which model has the largest horsepower?
sql: SELECT T1.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId  =  T2.Id WHERE T2.Cylinders  =  4 ORDER BY T2.horsepower DESC LIMIT 1;
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`car_1.cars_data`)-[:`car_1.cars_data_HAS_car_1.car_names`]->(T1:`car_1.car_names`)
WHERE T2.Cylinders = 4
RETURN T1.Model
ORDER BY T2.Horsepower DESC
LIMIT 1
text2sql_target: SELECT T1.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId  =  T2.Id WHERE T2.Cylinders  =  4 ORDER BY T2.horsepower DESC LIMIT 1;

WHERE T2.Cylinders = 4

ORDER BY T2.Horsepower DESC
LIMIT 1

input_text: Which models are lighter than 3500 but not built by the 'Ford Motor Company'?
question: Which models are lighter than 3500 but not built by the 'Ford Motor Company'?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.model FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Model  =  T2.Model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T3 ON T2.MakeId  =  T3.Id JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T4 ON T1.Maker  =  T4.Id WHERE T3.weight  <  3500 AND T4.FullName != 'Ford Motor Company';
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`car_1.car_names`)-[:`car_1.car_names_HAS_car_1.model_list`]->(T1:`car_1.model_list`),(T3:`car_1.cars_data`)-[:`car_1.cars_data_HAS_car_1.car_names`]->(T2:`car_1.car_names`),(T1:`car_1.model_list`)-[:`car_1.model_list_HAS_car_1.car_makers`]->(T4:`car_1.car_makers`)
WHERE T3.Weight < 3500 AND T4.FullName <> 'Ford Motor Company'
RETURN DISTINCT T1.Model
text2sql_target: SELECT DISTINCT T1.model FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Model  =  T2.Model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T3 ON T2.MakeId  =  T3.Id JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T4 ON T1.Maker  =  T4.Id WHERE T3.weight  <  3500 AND T4.FullName != 'Ford Motor Company';
text2cypher_target:
MATCH (T2:`car_1.car_names`)-[:`car_1.car_names_HAS_car_1.model_list`]->(T1:`car_1.model_list`),(T3:`car_1.cars_data`)-[:`car_1.cars_data_HAS_car_1.car_names`]->(T2:`car_1.car_names`),(T1:`car_1.model_list`)-[:`car_1.model_list_HAS_car_1.car_makers`]->(T4:`car_1.car_makers`)
WHERE T3.Weight < 3500 AND T4.FullName <> 'Ford Motor Company'
RETURN DISTINCT T1.Model

input_text: Which city has most number of arriving flights?
question: Which city has most number of arriving flights?
sql: SELECT T1.City FROM AIRPORTS AS T1 JOIN FLIGHTS AS T2 ON T1.AirportCode  =  T2.DestAirport GROUP BY T1.City ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`flight_2.flights`)-[:`flight_2.flights_HAS_flight_2.airports`]->(T1:`flight_2.airports`)
WITH T1.City as City, count(*) AS cnt
RETURN City
ORDER BY cnt DESC,City DESC
LIMIT 1

sql: SELECT name FROM shop WHERE number_products  >  (SELECT avg(number_products) FROM shop)
cypher:
MATCH (shop:`employee_hire_evaluation.shop`)
WITH avg(shop.Number_products) AS number_products
MATCH (shop:`employee_hire_evaluation.shop`)
WHERE  shop.Number_products >  number_products 
RETURN shop.Name
text2sql_target: SELECT name FROM shop WHERE number_products  >  (SELECT avg(number_products) FROM shop)
text2cypher_target:
MATCH (shop:`employee_hire_evaluation.shop`)
WITH avg(shop.Number_products) AS number_products
MATCH (shop:`employee_hire_evaluation.shop`)
WHERE  shop.Number_products >  number_products 
RETURN shop.Name

input_text: find the name of employee who was awarded the most times in the evaluation.
question: find the name of employee who was awarded the most times in the evaluation.
sql: SELECT t1.name FROM employee AS t1 JOIN evaluation AS t2 ON t1.Employee_ID  =  t2.Employee_ID GROUP BY t2.Employee_ID ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
cypher:
MATCH (t2:`employee_hire_evaluation.evaluation`)-[:`employee_hire_evaluation.evaluation_HAS_employee_hire_evaluation.employee`]->(t1:`employee_hire_evaluation.employee`)
WITH count(*) AS cnt, t1.Employee_ID as Employee_ID, t1.Name as Name
RETURN Name
ORDER BY cnt DESC,Employee_ID DESC
LIMIT 1
text2sql_target: SELECT t1.name FROM employee AS t1 JOIN evaluation AS t2 ON t1.Employee_ID  =  t2.Employee_ID GROUP BY t2.Employee_ID ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
text2cypher_target:
MATCH (t2:`employee_hire_evaluation.evaluation`)-[:`employee_hire_evaluation.evaluation_HAS_employee_hire_evaluation.employee`]->(t1:`employee_hire_evaluation.employee`)
WITH count(*) AS cnt, t1.Employee_ID as Employee_ID, t1.Name as Name
RETURN Name
ORDER BY cnt DESC,Employee_ID DESC
LIMIT 1

ORDER BY cnt DESC,Shop_ID DESC
LIMIT 1

input_text: What are the different ids and names of the battles that lost any 'Brig' type shipes?
question: What are the different ids and names of the battles that lost any 'Brig' type shipes?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.id ,  T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.ship_type  =  'Brig'
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`battle_death.ship`)-[:`battle_death.ship_HAS_battle_death.battle`]->(T1:`battle_death.battle`)
WHERE T2.ship_type = 'Brig'
RETURN DISTINCT T1.id,T1.name
text2sql_target: SELECT DISTINCT T1.id ,  T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle WHERE T2.ship_type  =  'Brig'

WHERE T2.ship_type = 'Brig'
RETURN DISTINCT T1.id,T1.name

input_text: What are the ids and names of the battles that led to more than 10 people killed in total.
question: What are the ids and names of the battles that led to more than 10 people killed in total.
sql: SELECT T1.id ,  T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id  =  T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING sum(T3.killed)  >  10
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`battle_death.ship`)-[:`battle_death.ship_HAS_battle_death.battle`]->(T1:`battle_death.battle`),(T3:`battle_death.death`)-[:`battle_death.death_HAS_battle_death.ship`]->(T2:`battle_death.ship`)
WITH T1.name AS name, sum(T3.killed) AS sum, T1.id AS id
WHERE sum > 10
RETURN id,name
text2sql_target: SELECT T1.id ,  T1.name FROM battle AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.lost_in_battle JOIN death AS T3 ON T2.id  =  T3.caused_by_ship_id GROUP BY T1.id HAVING sum(T3.killed)  >  10
text2cypher_target:
MATCH (T2:`battle_death.ship`)-[:`battle_death.ship_HAS_battle_death.battle`]->(T1:`battle_death.battle`),(T3:`battle_death.death`)-[:`battle_death.death_HAS_battle_death.ship`]->(T2:`battle_death.ship`)
WITH T1.name AS name, sum(T3.killed) AS sum, T1.id AS id
WHERE sum > 10
RETURN id,name

input_text: Which department offers the most number of degrees? List department name and id.
question: Which department offers the most number of degrees? List department name and id.
sql: SELECT T2.department_name ,  T1.department_id FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Departments AS T2 ON T1.department_id  =  T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
cypher:
MATCH (T1:`student_transcripts_tracking.Degree_Programs`)-[:`student_transcripts_tracking.degree_programs_HAS_student_transcripts_tracking.departments`]->(T2:`student_transcripts_tracking.Departments`)
WITH count(*) AS cnt, T2.department_name as department_name, T1.department_id as department_id
RETURN department_name,department_id
ORDER BY cnt DESC,department_id DESC
LIMIT 1
text2sql_target: SELECT T2.department_name ,  T1.department_id FROM Degree_Programs AS T1 JOIN Departments AS T2 ON T1.department_id  =  T2.department_id GROUP BY T1.department_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1

cypher:
MATCH (T2:`student_transcripts_tracking.Sections`)-[:`student_transcripts_tracking.sections_HAS_student_transcripts_tracking.courses`]->(T1:`student_transcripts_tracking.Courses`)
WITH T1.course_id AS course_id, T1.course_name AS course_name, count(*) AS count
WHERE count  <= 2
RETURN course_name,course_id

input_text: Who are enrolled in 2 degree programs in one semester? List the first name, middle name and last name and the id.
question: Who are enrolled in 2 degree programs in one semester? List the first name, middle name and last name and the id.
sql: SELECT T1.first_name ,  T1.middle_name ,  T1.last_name ,  T1.student_id FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id  =  T2.student_id GROUP BY T1.student_id HAVING count(*)  =  2
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`student_transcripts_tracking.Student_Enrolment`)-[:`student_transcripts_tracking.student_enrolment_HAS_student_transcripts_tracking.students`]->(T1:`student_transcripts_tracking.Students`)
WITH T1.last_name AS last_name, T1.student_id AS student_id, T1.middle_name AS middle_name, count(*) AS count, T1.first_name AS first_name

text2sql_target: SELECT T1.Name ,  T2.Orchestra FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID

input_text: Show the names of conductors that have conducted more than one orchestras.
question: Show the names of conductors that have conducted more than one orchestras.
sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID GROUP BY T2.Conductor_ID HAVING COUNT(*)  >  1
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`orchestra.orchestra`)-[:`orchestra.orchestra_HAS_orchestra.conductor`]->(T1:`orchestra.conductor`)
WITH T1.Conductor_ID AS Conductor_ID, T1.Name AS Name, count(*) AS count
WHERE count  > 1
RETURN Name
text2sql_target: SELECT T1.Name FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID GROUP BY T2.Conductor_ID HAVING COUNT(*)  >  1

WITH T1.Conductor_ID AS Conductor_ID, T1.Name AS Name, count(*) AS count
WHERE count  > 1
RETURN Name

input_text: Show the name of the conductor that has conducted the most number of orchestras.
question: Show the name of the conductor that has conducted the most number of orchestras.
sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM conductor AS T1 JOIN orchestra AS T2 ON T1.Conductor_ID  =  T2.Conductor_ID GROUP BY T2.Conductor_ID ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1
cypher:
MATCH (T2:`orchestra.orchestra`)-[:`orchestra.orchestra_HAS_orchestra.conductor`]->(T1:`orchestra.conductor`)
WITH T1.Name as Name, count(*) AS cnt, T1.Conductor_ID as Conductor_ID
RETURN Name
ORDER BY cnt DESC,Conductor_ID DESC
LIMIT 1

input_text: Which owner owns the most dogs? List the owner id, first name and last name.
question: Which owner owns the most dogs? List the owner id, first name and last name.
sql: SELECT T1.owner_id ,  T2.first_name ,  T2.last_name FROM Dogs AS T1 JOIN Owners AS T2 ON T1.owner_id  =  T2.owner_id GROUP BY T1.owner_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
cypher:
MATCH (T1:`dog_kennels.Dogs`)-[:`dog_kennels.dogs_HAS_dog_kennels.owners`]->(T2:`dog_kennels.Owners`)
WITH count(*) AS cnt, T1.owner_id as owner_id, T2.first_name as first_name, T2.last_name as last_name
RETURN owner_id,first_name,last_name
ORDER BY cnt DESC,owner_id DESC
LIMIT 1

input_text: Show the names of singers that have more than one song.
question: Show the names of singers that have more than one song.

cypher:
MATCH (T2:`singer.song`)-[:`singer.song_HAS_singer.singer`]->(T1:`singer.singer`)
WITH T1.Name AS Name, count(*) AS count
WHERE count  > 1
RETURN Name

input_text: What are the names of properties that are either houses or apartments with more than 1 room?
question: What are the names of properties that are either houses or apartments with more than 1 room?
sql: SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code  =  "House" UNION SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code  =  "Apartment" AND room_count  >  1
cypher:
MATCH (properties:`real_estate_properties.Properties`)
WHERE properties.property_type_code = 'House'
RETURN properties.property_name  AS value 
UNION
MATCH (properties:`real_estate_properties.Properties`)
WHERE properties.property_type_code = 'Apartment' AND properties.room_count > 1
RETURN properties.property_name  AS value
text2sql_target: SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code  =  "House" UNION SELECT property_name FROM Properties WHERE property_type_code  =  "Apartment" AND room_count  >  1
text2cypher_target:
MATCH (properties:`real_estate_properties.Properties`)
WHERE properties.property_type_code = 'House'
RETURN properties.property_name  AS value 
UNION
MATCH (properties:`real_estate_properties.Properties`)
WHERE properties.property_type_code = 'Apartment' AND properties.room_count > 1
RETURN properties.property_name  AS value
